items.title: In Barrow Creek $5 hangs on the pub's wall, still waiting for Peter Falconio

children.content: Twenty-five years ago, the murder of British backpacker Peter Falconio and the abduction and escape of his girlfriend Joanne Lees in the remote Central Australian outback captured global attention. Today, it remains one of the nation's most infamous cases.

articles.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed weeks before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final weeks, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of British tourist Peter Falconio's remains.

image.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

gallery.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO
A woman sitting at the bar inside a dim hotel, looking over the mementos plastered on the walls around her.

thumbnail.altText: A old photo of couple sitting in the front seats of a Kombi van, they are turned around to look at the camera.
An Australian five dollar note stuck to a wall with other notes and papers reading PETER + PAUL FALCONIO
A woman sitting at the bar inside a dim hotel, looking over the mementos plastered on the walls around her.

items.title: Violence against women under national spotlight after four deaths in four days

children.content: Experts are calling for more research and better funding for domestic violence prevention after four women and girls were killed in separate incidents last week.

image.altText: A composite showing photos of three women, and a fourth picture that shows the shadow outline of a girl

items.title: Why are the world's biggest AI firms so attracted to the NT?

children.content: The NT's sunny skies, natural gas reserves and proximity to Asia have caught the attention of a dozen data centre developers.

image.altText: A computer-generated image of a huge industrial site in an outback area.

items.title: Shock resignation of NT children's commissioner deemed 'a tragedy'

children.content: Australia's peak advocate for Aboriginal children has described the circumstances around child protection in the Northern Territory as "dire", following the shock resignation of the Territory's children's commissioner.

articles.title: Children's commissioner quits as CLP set to pass controversial welfare changes

children.content: Shahleena Musk's resignation comes as the Northern Territory government-dominated scrutiny committee backs the Country Liberal Party's changes to child protection laws, despite widespread opposition and confusion about a key aspect of the legislation.

image.altText: A woman wearing a black dress looking directly at the camera.

gallery.altText: A woman, looking serious, leaning against a white wall covered in children's painted handprints in bright colours.

thumbnail.altText: A woman, looking serious, leaning against a white wall covered in children's painted handprints in bright colours.

avatar.alt: A journalist standing inside a television news studio, she is wearing a white blazer and smiling at the camera.

items.title: NT government facing Federal Court over 'draconian' compensation cap laws

children.content: The NT government's controversial laws capping compensation for prisoners unlawfully assaulted, strip searched or imprisoned are being challenged in the Federal Court.

image.altText: A building painted in black and brown reads: NAAJA.

items.title: Man's death 'not suspicious' after 'large fight' on APY Lands, police say

children.content: Police said the death of a 59-year-old man was not suspicious after a fight broke out on South Australia's APY Lands, but investigations into the disturbance are continuing.

image.altText: A drone of houses with mountain range behind them.

items.title: Crocs, sharks and an Aussie cricket legend: All the action from the Barra Classic

children.content: Crocs, sharks and an Australian cricket legend have all turned up for one of Australia's biggest barra fishing tournaments on the Daly River.

gallery.altText: Three fishers, two standing, one sitting on a boat, floating along green water.

thumbnail.altText: Three fishers, two standing, one sitting on a boat, floating along green water.

items.title: 'Jurassic family': Spielberg leads outpouring of tributes for actor Sam Neill

children.content: The New Zealand actor died suddenly at the age of 78, with Hollywood directors and actors and prominent political figures all paying tribute to a great of global cinema.

gallery.altText: Sam Neill in character as Dr Alan Grant in Jurassic Park, standing in the rain wearing an akubra.
Black and white image smiling Jeff Goldblum, Laura Dern and Sam Neill

items.title: 'I have everything to ruin your life': Sextortion threats on the rise

children.content: Major online platforms are accused of failing to deploy readily available technology to detect well-known blackmail tactics.

image.altText: Girl at computer with emojis and speech bubble reading 'Are you alone?'

items.title: Angus Taylor says he criticised One Nation with a 'heavy heart'

children.content: The Opposition Leader says he reluctantly made a speech criticising Pauline Hanson's One Nation party policies. Follow live.

image.altText: Angus Taylor

items.title: Oil posts biggest gain since 2020 as Middle East war fears reignite

children.content: Oil prices soar more than 9 per cent as the US president threatens to blockade the Strait of Hormuz. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A 3D printed oil pump jack is placed on dollar banknotes in this illustration picture

items.title: Ukraine and allies join to protect Europe from ballistic missiles

children.content: Ukraine and nine other countries will form a coalition to protect Europe from ballistic missiles, utilising Kyiv's experience in fighting Russia's full-scale invasion for over four years.

image.altText: A ballistic missile crashing towards the Kyiv skyline.

items.title: Man who sneaked into Australia 45 years ago has lived in limbo until now

children.content: Kwong Wan Lo stowed away on a ship bound for Australia 45 years ago. With no legal right to stay, he built a life in Sydney anyway.

gallery.altText: A portrait of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a black jacket.
A headshot of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a hat and sitting in an office.

A headshot of Kwong Wan Lo wearing a hat and sitting in an office.

items.title: Mixed martial arts group attempts to gift $82k Cognac to Brisbane Olympic committee

children.content: The "schmoozing", revealed exclusively by the ABC today, is part of a wider series of examples of attempted influence-peddling from businesses and sporting bodies before the Games kick off in 2032.

image.altText: a bottle of cognac next to two glasses on top of a barrell

items.title: Elon Musk's criticisms of The Odyssey ignore an important fact

children.content: The tech trillionaire has spent a good chunk of 2026 yelling on X about how Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey "desecrates" the ancient poet.

image.altText: Soliders in a forest

items.title: Jewish professor says 'intimidating' protest changed his life on campus

children.content: Melbourne University professor Steven Prawer hopes the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will lead to campus change, following a 2024 demonstration that saw protesters invade his office.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Your challenge in the ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

children.content: The process was simple and the service was speedy but the ease with which 7.30 acquired illicit tobacco products was concerning.

items.title: Police charge alleged coordinator of Chris Baghsarian kidnapping and murder

children.content: A man accused of coordinating the alleged murder and kidnapping of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian has been charged.

items.title: Signs of sugar detected near centre of the Milky Way

children.content: Astronomers have detected signs of a type of sugar found in raspberries in gas clouds near the centre of our galaxy the Milky Way.

items.title: Man dies after being pulled from burning caravan in central Qld

children.content: A man has died after a fire ripped through a caravan in central Queensland overnight.

items.title: Richard Scolyer encouraged Australians to enrol in research. Here's how

children.content: In his open letter to all Australians, the late Richard Scolyer wrote of the pride he felt taking part in clinical trials. But what exactly is it like to participate?

image.altText: A man sits at a desk and has an eye test by a female health worker.

items.title: 'Ovulating, come over now': The seedy side of online sperm donation

children.content: Australia is facing a sperm donation shortage and it is leading those desperate to find a way to conceive down some expensive and potentially dangerous paths.

items.title: Former Victorian Young Australian of the Year charged over imitation guns

children.content: Darcy McGauley-Bartlett has been arrested and charged with a raft of offences, including possessing imitation firearms and police uniforms.

image.altText: man standing in suit holding award, looking proud

items.title: Probe finds sport club's ex-president awarded $450K of work to own company

children.content: An investigation by the NSW Independent Liquor and Gaming Authority has resulted in former Erina Rugby League Football Club president James Savage being banned and fined over conflicts of interest.

image.altText: A sign for the Erina Leagues Club.

items.title: Man charged with murder over death of 74yo Sydney man

children.content: A man has been charged with murder over the death of an elderly man who was allegedly attacked during a morning walk in Sydney's west.

image.altText: a close up and full length police image of forty five year old corum ash over a warrant for attempted murder

items.title: De Minaur ends 17-year Australian drought with career-high ranking

children.content: Alex de Minaur's arrow keeps pointing upwards despite his wait for a grand slam title being lengthened by a disappointing Wimbledon exit.

image.altText: A man holds his racquet up to a tennis crowd.

items.title: 'Gatekeeping septuagenarians': 'Inappropriate' TikTok video deleted

children.content: The National Trust of SA says one of its volunteers apologised after posting a social media clip labelling a group of disaffected members "gatekeeping septuagenarians" who were running a "stuffy museum".

image.altText: "Sex is great... but have you ever experienced the euphoria of ripping down every single no photo sign in a stuffy museum?"

items.title: Yemen's Houthis target Saudi airport with ballistic missiles and drones

children.content: The Iran-aligned Houthi movement that controls northern Yemen has accused Saudi Arabia of launching air strikes against the international airport in Sanaa.

image.altText: A smoke cloud seen above buildings.

items.title: Video appears to show Thailand fire 'flashover' event, expert says

children.content: A video analysis of an explosive pub fire in Thailand, which killed 27 people, appears to show a flashover "point of no return" that would have made evacuation nearly impossible, an expert says.

image.altText: Flames erupt from pub entrance at nighttime as patrons flee onto street.

items.title: New pokies policies will 'make things worse' for problem gamblers

children.content: The body tasked with implementing Tasmania's new pokies policies says they won't work to reduce harm to problem gamblers and could make things worse. The Liquor and Gaming Commission was not consulted on the changes, which include ATMs in gaming venues.

image.altText: A man sitting in front of a poker machine. Two more poker machines are next to him

items.title: International buyers hit outback markets to hunt for Australia's best opals

children.content: The outback town of Winton is known for dinosaur fossils and as the location of the first performance of Waltzing Matilda, but has also become an important part of a growing international luxury trade.

image.altText: a person's fingertips tightly holding a small iridescent opal

items.title: Caution over radical social media bushwalk plans

children.content: Don't plan a bushwalk based on what an Instagram influencer may have supposedly achieved, a veteran rescuer says, ahead of Tasmania's winter season approaching.
